item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream Was Reportedly Feature-Complete A Year Ago
item.content:encoded:
The USK is at it again

Have you all been living the dream this weekend? If you've been playing Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream, then you likely have! Stuffed full of hilarious possibilities and an endless amount of creativity, it's been the meme of the weekend.

And good friend of the site Jon Cartwright highlighted some interesting news about the life sim's development: the game may have been ready almost a year ago, if a USK rating is to be believed.

According to the rating, the game was classified on 20th May 2025, meaning it was at least ready for play-testing and basically done, save a few final things.

This isn't anything new for Nintendo; over the years, there have been frequent reports that the company has sat on game reveals and announcements for games that are already complete.

Metroid Prime Remastered, for example, was reportedly rated by the USK almost two years before it was released. Fire Emblem Engage was also a similar story. Those are just two examples, but it's happened a number if times.

Nintendo may have had to do some last-minute tweaks on Living the Dream between the May 2025 rating and the release, but essentially, this rating implies that the Big N had the space to find a slot for the game that worked.

It also meant the team likely wasn't scrambling to get the game done on time, which is always a good thing. And that seems to have been reflected in the reviews and online feedback from fans, who are having a blast making people laugh online with their ridiculous creations.

Are you enjoying Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream on Switch? Want to see anything added to the game? Let us know in the comments.

[source usk.de, via bsky.app]

Alana has been with Nintendo Life since 2022, and while RPGs are her first love, Nintendo is a close second. She enjoys nothing more than overthinking battle strategies, characters, and stories. She also wishes she was a Sega air pirate.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.title: Neon Odyssey, Avantris' New 5.5e Sourcebook "is fully space opera with a heavy coat of nostalgic paint and neon vibes" - Interview
item.content:encoded:
Hot on the heels of the release of The Crooked Moon, showcasing Daggerheart and continuing to release their latest actual play, Once Upon A Starlight, The Legends of Avantris are just about to launch a new Kickstarter; Neon Odyssey. This D&D Sourcebook is 1400+ pages of worlds, creatures, items, missions, and more to fuel DMs worlds filled with Space Opera goodness.

Neon Odyssey launches on Kickstarter on May 5th, ahead of its release we had a chance to sit down with Avantris' Co-founder and Head Of Creative and Marketing, Mikey Gilder, to talk about what Neon Odyssey is, how this was just one of multiple ideas being cooked up at Avantris, and how much there is in store for backers.

Neon Odyssey Takes Dungeons & Dragons To The Stars

Gilder kicked off our discussion by breaking down just what Neon Odyssey is and just how large a project it is. "Neon Odyssey is our follow up to The Crooked Moon, it is a 1400+ page Space Opera trilogy for 5.5e Dungeons & Dragons. You know it's funny, it's 1,400 pages and I laugh, but the amount that we've had to cut and reduce in scope in order to hit 1,400 pages is pretty immense."

"Effectively it's just a massive trilogy that brings Space Opera to D&D. We needed a galaxy so we needed three books. It's comprised of our character book, which is called the Outrunner's Handbook, then there is our setting book which is called the Cosmic Codex, and then finally there is kinda like an expansion pack that has a bunch of stuff that are expanded systems and that's called the Overdrive Expansion."

"We really think no matter whether you're a player, a GM, anyone in between you're going to have something to enjoy."

I had to compliment the Avantris team on over doubling their page count from The Crooked Moon, which is already a very hefty book. A description of the contents of the books on their pre-release website highlights that the scope of Neon Odyssey will include information on 30 worlds, 6 interdimensional realms, and 13 factions.

Many Lifetimes Of Learning From The Crooked Moon

As to when Neon Odyssey will reach the hands of backers after the May Kickstarter Gilder let me know they're playing it a bit closer to the chest currently but it will be on the Kickstarter page. Something that he did want to reassure fans and backers of is just how much they learned during the fulfilment period of The Crooked Moon.

"A lot of the mistakes and pains we had from when it came to manufacturing, when it came to fulfillment, writing, editing, and getting everything done we've learned several lifetimes worth of lessons from The Crooked Moon. [Neon Odyssey] is a massive project, there's still a good amount to do, a ton of it is written, we've been working on it for over two years now in various stages of development. We're optimistic that we'll be able to have it be a lot smoother than The Crooked Moon."

The core concept of Neon Odyssey is to take Dungeons & Dragons to a vaporwave sci-fi setting. Knowing the game's traditional roots in High Fantasy, and Avantris' dive into Folk Horror I was interested to learn where this colorful take on TTRPG originated from.

"The way that we've been deciding projects is that we don't do any kind of market research. Thinking about what will sell the best or looking at the competitive field. What we really do is think about what do we want to play? What do we want to see?"

"When we sat down to decide what our next project would be we sat down with four different thematic concepts." before I could even ask Gilder immediately continued "I can't talk about the other three."

"After a few bits of discussion with the core team [Neon Odyssey] was a no brainer. It was the thing that made us the most passionate, got the ideas turning the most. It's also something that we want to play." In a world where so much entertainment is overly market tested or crafted by committee for marketing at the detriment of heart/passion I was very excited to hear that this was how Avantris was choosing their projects.

Gilder brought up his history in the field of marketing and how his own title at Avantris is Head of Marketing but that "that way I see it is 'what's going to make us excited and passionate, and excited to show off?' and that's typically, at least what we've found, is what people are excited to show up and support… and it's what's worked so far… knock on wood."

Join The Pre-Kickstarter To Lock In Free Dice And Get Early Access Content

For those who can't wait for the Kickstarter to launch there's also a VIP program that you can already join at NeonRPG.com.

This VIP program, that's been active since the announcement of Neon Odyssey and will be available until the launch of the Kickstarter, is a $1 reservation to get access to a variety of benefits. "You unlock a bunch of perks. You get immediate access to the Neon Odyssey Discord with tons of sneak peeks. We've been revealing species, subclasses, milestones, chat with the Avantris crew. You also get early access to our massive playtest document which is already over 50 pages of Neon Odyssey including our entire starship combat system."

For those who are part of the VIP Program, once the Kickstarter has launched and you back it "you'll immediately get a digital adventure featuring Chuckles The Space Clown and then if you back anything physical you'll get a free set of VIP exclusive Neon Nights Dice that are super synth-wave. You've got your cyan and magenta really fun moody vibes. The only way to get that is to be a VIP"

Gilder also explained that there have been VIP stretch goals that have been unlocking during this pre-campaign period. At our time of talking they were already well on-pace to unlock their final stretch goal at 30,000 VIPs. These aren't including any of the main campaign stretch goals. On that topic Gilder simply told me that when the Kickstarter says 40+ subclasses, 30+ Species, etc that the 'plus' is doing a lot of heavy lifting.

D&D In Space, But Fresh

To ensure the Avantris take on D&D in Space was unique to them Gilder broke down the way that he most commonly saw the pairing made. "I think a lot of times Sci-fi in D&D goes in two pretty clear directions. One is a lot more 'Hey, it's High Fantasy in Outer Space. You've got galleons with canvas flying through space' on the other side you have 'Here's our take on Mass Effect and the vibes of Mass Effect' and we didn't want to do that."

"We wanted to say 'hey, our version of sci-fi space opera is fully space opera with a heavy coat of nostalgic paint and neon vibes'"

This coat of paint is being applied to the classes of Dungeons & Dragons where players familiar with the Wizard might instead roll up a Gridrunner, or a Barbarian can try their hand at being a Juggernaut. New sub-classes for the theme are also being added. An Icon (Bard) can tour the planets as a Holostar and join with a Tracer (Ranger) Powertech.

Asking what it was like to stare down the barrel of 5.5e character creation and know that pretty much everything needed to be reskinned Gilder simply responded "It was daunting."

"As we set out we kept peeling back the layers of how much we had to do. The last thing we wanted was to say 'I'm playing a Paladin or a Wizard… but in outer space.' If you want to play our version of the Scoundrel and call your ability Sneak Attack that's fine, but for the people who want to be fully immersed we wanted the option to have abilities like Cheap Shot so when you're looking at the book it's a fully immersive, comprehensively thematic product"

Classes Recontextualized

Gilder did highlight that while a lot has been reskinned and renamed to better match the theme mechanically everything is very similar. "We wanted the play to be very familiar and not obtuse." That being said there are some concepts that are reconceptualized. Using the Gridrunner (Wizard) as an example Gilder explained that instead of the Weave there is the Hypergrid and the Gridrunners are able to code it.

"The Hypergrid is this digital realm that overlays the entire galaxy – It's kinda like the Grid from TRON and kinda like The Matrix – It's this digital world and [the Gridrunners] code it. They are the Software Engineers, able to manipulate reality with their coding."

Other changes that you'll see in classes is that the Cryptist (Warlock) as a class didn't function as patrons aren't really a thing so instead the Cryptist deal in hidden secrets of the world and learning secrets that unlock their minds. The Juggernaut (Barbarian) doesn't tap into primal energies, but they instead have a pool of kinetic power that allows them to push their bodies further than previously possible.

"We have an incredible internal team, we have a huge roster of writers this time around who have all contributed amazing things, but our lead designer Dan Dillan – Avantris Entertainment's Lead Game Designer – he's been shoulder to shoulder with the rest of us, really tackling all of these problems."

As Gilder explained the ways that a Juggernaut's 'Surge' gives them access to different elemental abilities, and how the Mechanist (Artificer) can discover different blueprints that alter the way that their powers grow we got to talking about how a lot of the systems in Neon Odyssey aren't just to influence the path of a character when they level up, but they're also provided with choices along the way.

Optional Systems To Layer On Your Character

Gilder explained that with the Overdrive Expansion there are additional optional progression systems that they came up with. "One of the things that Neon Odyssey has is optional systems like our Profession system. If you want to be a Smuggler, an Ace Pilot, those kinds of science fiction space opera archetypes we didn't want to have locked behind any class so the Professions are something you can overlay as a track of feats that you can help to have your character feel like the classic archetypes of heroes."

"Systems like those, even as we approach sub-class design and species design, Avantris opted more towards building it for experienced GMs. It's more complex because it's a galaxy that we had to build. One of the things that I really like is more lateral power, I like more options. Every time you're playing your character you have multiple things to do. As opposed to 'I have one thing I can do' or 'I have one passive feature that might come up, and that's my whole thing for a level'"

"You're not getting a lot of stuff you can stack in vertical power, but you have more options to make you feel deeply immersed" - Gilder speaking on Avantris' focus on lateral power

Cosmic Codex Will Give GMs The Gist Of A World

Moving away from the different player options of Neon Odyssey I was interested to learn what information will be given to those running the games.

"1,400 pages sounds like a lot but when you have as many concepts as we have we have to be pretty judicious. What we're trying to do is equip GMs with a great number of locations, adventuring opportunities, and basically barrels of TNT that they can choose to light."

Gilder described the offerings to the GM as a cafeteria of options that they can pull from all manner of resources to build up a campaign that will best fit their table and theme. "Every single time we create a world or faction we want the theme to be extremely clear."

In the world creation process Gilder explained that they didn't want to provide information on it being temperate and describing something that's basically Earth. "That's great, but that's not as useful in our eyes. Instead here's our frozen world, our volcano world, here's our nasty swamp world, here's our world that's been taken over by a bunch of demonic bugs like Gears of War or Starship Troopers."

"We want to offer you to tools to create an archetypical sci-fi adventure, we have done the work for you to just inject that theme that you can flesh out"

Returning to the idea that these books, even at 1,400 pages, had to be truncated and edited down to efficiently fit everything that they are covering Gilder commented "shoot, I wish I had four, five, six more honestly." Asking whether that was content ready for an expansion in the future Gilder agreed "If it goes well and people want to come back to this galaxy we've got many years ahead."

Listing off some of the sci-fi storylines that Avantris wants to make sure people can experience there's the tools for "if you want to play a group of bounty hunters down on their luck like Cowboy Bebop, if you want to play your whimsical saturday morning cartoon-style with a retro vibe, or if you want to play your classic farmboy or real common folk that gets whipped up into galactic conflict we want you to be able to do that."

"We want to make sure we have the tools and the handholds to play and design the kind of space opera stories you want"

Kickstarter Benefits and Stretch Goals

It has already been advertised that as part of the Kickstarter players will be able to get their hands on an oracle deck, miniatures of the creatures featured in the books, and a number of sets of dice. I wanted to know what else people should be getting excited for.

"People who know me know I'm a plushie guy, I love plusies. The Borb, the fat round crow from The Crooked Moon, was a runaway hit and I think that it would be foolish for us to not do plushies again. That will be something that hopefully people find very cute."

"What we're also offering is a couple dozen ship miniatures for our starship scale combat. They're all made for 1-Inch hexes and we're offering a run of a bunch of different starship designs. We're going to have two boxes of personal standard starfighters which are like an X-wing or an Arwing. A box of mechs if you want to do a mech and then also a box of transport miniatures in the style of the Millennium Falcon, the Serenity, the Bebop."

As someone whose printed a few of The Crooked Moon miniatures for use in my home game I was also able to confirm that while you can purchase these ships as printed miniatures there will also be the chance to get STLs and print them at home if you have a 3D printer.

Talking more about the tiers and bundling options Gilder also wanted to highlight that a lot of work was done to try to give even more value to the box tiers. "Our core box, deluxe box, and all in box have as much savings as possible. If you opt to support us at the highest tier you're going to be saving nearly $2,000."



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/features/neon-odyssey-avantris-new-55e-sourcebook-is-fully-space-opera-with-heavy-coat-of

item.title: The Sonic 4 Trailer May Contain A Hint About A Fifth Movie, Actually
item.content:encoded:
Paramount showed off a brief look at Sonic the Hedgehog 4 at Cinemacon last week, and while the short trailer has not been shared publicly, we have been told that it showed Jim Carrey returning as Robotnik. However, this presentation may have also contained a clue about the future of the Sonic live-action movies beyond the fourth movie coming out next year. At least, if a long-standing theory is shown to hold water a fourth time.

Tika Sumpter plays Maddie Wachowski, a vet and Sonic’s adoptive human mother in the movies. While she was primarily a supporting character in the beginning, her role has grown a bit over the course of the first three movies, to the point where fans have warmed up to her and the rest of the new human cast. However, she also has, whether intentionally or not, ended up becoming a major point of speculation for future Sonic movies because at some point, whether it be in the movie itself or in promotional material, she seems to wear a shirt that corresponds to the Sonic character that eventually shows up in the post-credits scene of the movie. 

This isn’t just a one-off, either. Sumpter has worn shirts that lined up with all three post-credits teasers in the first three films. In the first movie, she sported a yellow tanktop, and then Sonic’s fox friend Tails showed up at the end. During the second film’s climax, she wears a red shirt and black jacket, and Shadow the Hedgehog was teased after the credits. Lastly, in 2024’s Sonic the Hedgehog 3, Maddie had on a pink sweater during the family’s camping trip, and then Amy Rose swooped in to rescue Sonic in the closing scene. Now that a pattern has been established, Sonic fans are on the lookout for Sumpter’s fit in every shot, piece of promo material, or, in the case of Cinemacon, in a video message featuring the actor and James Marsden, who plays her character’s husband, Tom.

what color was her shirt https://t.co/9L62Bq6QW8

— Frack's Sonic Deals (@FrackDeals) April 17, 2026

Cinemacon has notoriously tight security and doesn’t allow filming, so most of the information we get from the Las Vegas show comes from people who were there, and so far, no one has spilled the beans on what color Sumpter’s shirt was in the message. We’ve reached out to some folks who were at the event to see if we can find out what color she was wearing and will update this story if we hear back.

Now that fans have latched onto this theory, I wouldn’t be surprised if Paramount has Sumpter start dressing in misleading colors to throw fans off. Or they might play into it since it gets people talking. Personally I’m crossing my fingers for a white and purple top so Rouge the Bat finally shows up when Sonic the Hedgehog 4 comes to theaters on March 19, 2027.



https://kotaku.com/sonic-hedgehog-4-tika-sumpter-maddy-shirt-amy-shadow-2000689074
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Forza Horizon 6′s Xbox Controller Is A Hideous Neon Mess And I Love It
item.content:encoded:
Some official video game-themed controllers look amazing. Some look ugly and boring. And then, every once in a while, you get something so garish and neon, like Xbox’s newly revealed Forza Horizon 6 gamepad, that it’s almost hard to look at it. And you know what? I want this damn Forza controller so bad.

On April 20, Xbox revealed a new Forza Horizon 6-themed controller and wireless headset. The controller is translucent blue and covered in neon pink and yellow shapes and lines, as if my windbreaker from the ’90s got sick and puked all over it. The headset is a bit less garish, but still features a lot of neon shapes and letters. It almost looks like something I’d find in Marathon as I was looting for some rods or whatever.

Both the controller and headset are available for pre-order now. The controller costs $90, and the headset costs $135. They’ll be released on May 18.  Meanwhile, Forza Horizon 6 arrives on Xbox and PC on May 19. Here’s our preview of the game, which moves the franchise to Japan.

Some Zack lore for you: I always wanted the Forza Horizon 5 Xbox controller. It was a translucent yellow with splashes of different neon colors all over it. The thing looked equally amazing and horrific. I never got my hands on it for various reasons. But this time around, I’m not going to miss out. I’m going to buy this Forza Horizon 6 controller and place it next to my amazing Marathon PS5 controller and be happy that I’m an adult who is in a place in my life where I can afford such neon nonsense. That’s the American Dream, baby.



https://kotaku.com/forza-horizon-6-new-xbox-controller-neon-headset-price-release-date-pc-2000689054

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase Announced
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has officially announced that the reveal showcase for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will arrive this week on April 23.

After months of anticipation, countless leaks and rumors, and endless discussions about figurines and artbooks, the moment has finally arrived to discover what Ubisoft has been working on. The highly anticipated remake of Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag will be revealed to the world this month.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Reveal Showcase Date and How to Watch

A new post on the official Ubisoft X account (formerly Twitter) confirms that the reveal showcase for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will be held on April 23, 2026. Interested readers can tune into the YouTube livestream at 4 PM UTC/6 PM CEST/9 AM PDT. Ubisoft has also poked fun at all the leaks and rumors that have arrived ahead of the remake’s official reveal, calling it “gaming’s worst kept secret” in the announcement teaser.

We still don’t know what will be included in the reveal showcase, but players will be expecting a first look at gameplay and, hopefully, a release date. Talking about the release, Insider Gaming exclusively confirmed earlier this week that the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake will launch on July 9, 2026. On the same day, we also saw the listing for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced briefly appear on Ubisoft’s launcher.

Regarding the Black Flag Remake, the Indonesian rating for the game confirms that players will receive new content. Ubisoft is looking for generative AI experience in their new hires, with multiple listings requiring this qualification. Will you be tuning in for the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced reveal showcase? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Naughty Dog almost shutting down Uncharted 4 because it ‘sucked’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Reign of Guilds Reduces Team Size & Server Merges Coming, But Development Continues
item.content:encoded:
Reign of Guilds developer Atlant Games now has just two developers on the game after cost-cutting measures. They’re also merging servers this week and have announced details on that process and future development goals.

Also due to cost-cutting, the server merges will turn CIS, EU, and NA into one global server on Wednesday, April 22nd, during the usual scheduled maintenance. The team shared details on what to know, including in case of duplicate names, if you have multiple characters, if you had characters on different servers, and you can check those out in the announcement on Discord.

There are some shifts with this change to be aware of as well. Kingdom of Dvorran will be split between two data centers, the ones that hosted CIS and NA. Oer County will be hosted on North American servers, including the castles in Oer County. If you own castles there, they should remain as they were, but in other counties those castles will remain as they were in the CIS region.

Along with all of these cost cutting measures as well as the team only being down to two developers, they postponed the decision to create a new server. However, there are upcoming updates in the works. The next update will include some balance changes as well as some ux improvements. While the team has been reduced in size, they are still planning anti-cheat, you ex changes, simplified mechanics, some quest changes, and they're working on reducing the concerns over the artificial difficulty curve. 

Atlant Games' studio head recently stepped down, though it is unclear if this is related. Reign of Guilds was launched out of Early Access last year.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/reign-of-guilds-reduces-team-size-and-server-merges-coming-but-development-continues-2000137849

item.title: Xbox Project Helix Has 0 GPU Customization, Leak Claims, Abandoning the Last Thing That Made an Xbox Feel Like One
item.content:encoded:
• 0-20%: Unlikely - Lacks credible sources
  • 21-40%: Questionable - Some concerns remain
  • 41-60%: Plausible - Reasonable evidence
  • 61-80%: Probable - Strong evidence
  • 81-100%: Highly Likely - Multiple reliable sources

Although little is currently known about the next-generation Xbox Project Helix, it's clear the system will be quite different from the Xbox One and the Xbox Series X|S, as it is set to bridge the gap between PC and console gaming. How this will be achieved remains to be seen, but in terms of hardware, according to known AMD leaker KeplerL2, Microsoft has decided to abandon the last thing that made an Xbox feel like an Xbox: custom hardware.

"MS has 0 customization on the GPU side this time," the leaker said on the NeoGAF forums in a thread discussing the next-generation Microsoft console and how it will compare to the PlayStation 6, which is set to bring a significant improvement over the base PS5, but not a massive one like the 10x RT performance improvement mentioned in AMD's leaked documents.

The lack of customization on the GPU side of the system, however, won't prevent Xbox Project Helix from running the AMD FSR Diamond family of upscaling, denoising, and frame generation transformer models built for RDNA 5, KeplerL2 highlighted.

While this is something that won't matter much to the general user, Xbox Project Helix doing away with any GPU customization feels like the end of an era. In the past, every console featured customized hardware to some degree, which helped each system stand out from the competition.

However, with the console looking increasingly poised to target the PC market, it definitely feels like this hardware choice will make it easier than ever for developers to port games to the system, if ports are actually required, while foregoing one of the things that makes a console one. Last month, a reliable leaker said the system is basically a PC that emulates the console experience and, as such, it would have little need for custom hardware.

With the "not-E3" season coming up and the system reportedly still targeting a late 2027 release window, there's a chance Microsoft will talk more about Xbox Project Helix in the coming months. Hopefully, we will also learn at what price the 5x rasterization and 20x ray tracing performance gains over the Xbox Series X will come.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-project-helix-0-gpu-customization/

item.title: This week in PC games: the "final" Molyneux game, Game Boy horror, ski resort management, and a glamcore heist sim
item.content:encoded:
What we're feeding the Maw

Happy this week, all! I return from my holiday in the mountains with spirit restored, skin bronzed, and knees destroyed and reforged into coiled springs of purest Adventure. Such wonders are my knees today! I rejoice in their knobbly tenacity and tensile splendour. I go out into the street in my pants and laugh, laugh at all the pathetic urban weaklings with quadriceps like overboiled spaghetti that could not bestir a butterfly's wing. I line up this week's new PC games and shoe them into the Maw like sparkling meteors.

Monday 20th April

  • Out this day in early access, Space Drilling Station is a vertical outpost builder I find strangely cosy, and not just because your cutaway dollhouse rig is right in the middle of a magma pool.
  • TownsFolk is a hexagonal survival strategy game about rebuilding a home in the wilds. There's a choice of turn-based or real-time play.

Tuesday 21st April

  • The Third Shift is a Game Boy-style horror game in which you are a museum guard, patrolling such exhibits as the Human Body and Terrors of the Deep. I do not remember playing any horror games on an actual Game Boy. Did you?
  • The old world is dying, and the new struggles to be born: now is the time of licensed genre crossovers. Vampire Crawlers is (deep breath) a turn-based first-person dungeon deckbuilder roguelite with characters and weaponry from Vampire Survivors.
  • Legendary Pilots is a flight sim with a sepia pixelart finish that approximately hails from the golden age of passenger aviation. Seriously, where are my Pan Am nerds.

Wednesday 22nd April

  • Tides of Tomorrow is "plasticpunk" Waterworld with zero Kevin Costners and some novel online functionality that sees you "story-linking" to other players. Their choices shape events in your post-apocalyptic world, and you can witness the ghosts of their decisions.
  • Masters of Albion is the new early access god sim from a bunch of former Lionhead and Bullfrog developers, amongst them Peter Molyneux, who says it's his final project. I have genuinely run out of emotions for Peter Molyneux. I gaze upon Masters of Albion with frigid tranquillity.
  • While we're reminiscing about 90s game development, Factory 95 is a factory game set inside a Windows OS from an era when Windows wasn't quite as eager to devour your soul. It has its own version of Clippy. Ah, dear, sweet Clippy – may flights of angels sing you to your rest.

Thursday 23rd April

  • Titanium Court is "a surreal strategy game for clowns and criminals." But doctor! I AM Titanium Court.
  • Offering App is a violent introspective sojourn and minigame package with lots of frightful hand-drawn art.
  • Rollick N Roll is a collection of near-edibly pleasant 3D marble runs, where you push buttons on the toy to make little trains move around.
  • Above the Snow is possibly the first alpine ski resort management game I've laid eyes on. You get to design your own hiking trails! Argh, my knees are cramping up in agonised recollection.

Friday 24th April

  • In Snap & Grab (pictured), you're a burglar masquerading as a glamorous fashion photographer. Take pictures of posh places to create an action plan for your heist team. Kind of like a no-kills Hitman playthrough, but Agent 47 wishes he had this hair.

Things being done by the RPS team this week include: hands-on impressions of [EMBARGOED]; hot chat about Pragmata; much ado about god sims; probably some piracy, because everything's a pirate game nowadays. Needless to say, the other editors are awash with knee-envy. Don't worry, everybody – give it a few days at my desk and my hamstrings will once again have the consistency of antique liquorice.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/this-week-in-pc-games-the-final-molyneux-game-game-boy-horror-ski-resort-management-and-a-glamcore-heist-sim

item.title: Vampire Crawlers Is A Spin-Off That’s Better Than The Original
item.content:encoded:
Remember the classic Lays potato chip slogan: “Betcha can’t eat just one?” It was quite a claim, but for most people, it was accurate. I feel confident that Vampire Crawlers, an official card-based spin-off of Poncle’s Vampire Survivors developed by Nosebleed Interactive, could make a similar claim in its marketing. I doubt most people who play Vampire Crawlers will be able to do just one run and walk away. Instead, they’ll do one more, and then another. And then maybe one more. And then, they’ll suddenly realize it’s 2 am on a work night and go to bed. Right after you do one more quick run…

Launching April 21 on PC, PS5, Switch, Switch 2, Xbox, and Game Pass, Vampire Crawlers is a card-based rogue-like dungeon crawler set in the same universe as the auto-shooter main game that quickly became a hit back in 2023. So if you’ve played Survivors, you’ll spot a lot of familiar enemies, power-ups, abilities, and characters as you work your way through the game’s many hand-crafted dungeons. But Vampire Crawlers isn’t a top-down auto-shooter built around constant, never-ending action. Instead, Crawlers is a slower-paced adventure in which players explore various levels step-by-step via a first-person perspective while battling through groups of enemies using a deck of cards that they improve and expand during each dungeon run.

What the heck is Vampire Crawlers?

As you explore each of the game’s dungeon mazes, you level up by killing enemies and collecting crystals, like in the main game. This will unlock new cards. And as you explore more, you unlock ways to upgrade these cards or even combine them into more-powerful variants. All of these cards, many of which do damage but can also trigger other effects and bonuses, have a mana cost to play them. The cost ranges from zero to five or more, depending on various factors. You only have so much mana to use each “turn” during a battle. So you play a few cards, and once you’ve used up all your mana, played all your cards in your hand, or have no other moves, your turn is over. If there are still enemies standing, they get to attack, dealing an amount of damage that is listed above the group. This repeats until either you die or you kill all the enemies that make up that encounter.

During my first runs in Crawlers, I just grabbed cards randomly or based on how powerful (or cool) they seemed. I was running on vibes, basically. This let me get through some early levels, but soon, I was getting killed before I could reach the end, or the final boss would trounce me. The key is to think more strategically.

Sure, you can play a 1 mana cost card and then a zero mana cost card. But you should play the zero mana card first, then the one cost card after, as this will begin to build a combo. The higher the combo gets, the more powerful your cards become. For example, some of my favorite cards let me draw more cards. These types of cards become far more useful when you play them later in a combo, as they will draw even more cards than normal. The same is true for cards that deal damage or give you extra mana. Building big combos and knowing when to play specific cards, like ones that can give you armor and help you absorb a big wave of damage, is the key to getting deeper into Crawlers.

It’s a very different gameplay experience compared to the auto-shooter action of Survivors, but I think I enjoy it more. Don’t get me wrong, Vampire Survivors is still a magnificent game that will never be removed from my Steam Deck. But the slower-paced action and more methodical exploration and combo-making in Crawlers tickles parts of my brain that Survivors never did, and it’s just far easier to play for hours and hours. Or until my Steam Deck dies. Bonus points: Crawlers‘ slower-paced card gameplay makes it a perfect game to play while watching YouTube. A dangerous combo for me in particular.

Crawlers is just as chaotic and exciting as Survivors

At first, when I started playing Vampire Crawlers, I was concerned that this shift to a slower-paced dungeon-crawler with cards would mean I’d not get to experience my favorite part of Survivors. You know, that moment when a run is going so well that you become a powerful nuclear bomb of monster-killin’ energy, and the screen is filled with endless particles as dozens of sound effects play at once and waves of baddies disintegrate in seconds. Thankfully, that can happen in Crawlers too, and it’s just as wonderful. Again, the key is to build up combos. Eventually, I was turning my screen into a pixelated mess of flame, electricity, and more enemy-smiting goodness as dozens of foes got melted in seconds. It was amazing and felt so dam satisfying.

The music and sound effects, many heavily inspired by or directly ripped from the main game, helped make these big combo moments all the better. And yes, the slot-machine-like music that plays when opening a chest in Vampire Survivors is also heard when opening chests found in the dungeon.

Between dungeon runs, you return to a hub-like town which acts as the game’s main menu. Here is where you’ll take the gold you find during runs and receive for finishing dungeons and use it to upgrade your stats permanently, much like you do in Vampire Survivors. There are also different characters to pick and play as, offering different card bonuses and effects. And there’s even a strange tent you unlock later that contains powerful passive abilities that can greatly change how you play and build decks during runs.

Even after playing around 13 hours of Vampire Crawlers, mostly on my Steam Deck (where it ran perfectly), I am still making fresh discoveries and unlocking more parts of the game. Like the main game, this is not something you’ll just play for a few hours and leave behind. Vampire Survivors is a game that people will be playing for weeks, months, or more, trying to get through dungeons without taking damage, using every character, or finding every secret. At least, that’s what I’ll be doing for the rest of the year.



https://kotaku.com/vampire-crawlers-review-vampire-survivors-steam-deck-spin-off-card-impresions-steam-2000689022

item.title: The End Of April Is Absolutely Stacked On Xbox Game Pass
item.content:encoded:
It feels like Microsoft left it a bit late to reveal the second half of April’s Game Pass releases, given that there’s only a third of the month left, but it seems the final week and a half is absolutely stacked with new additions, and many of them are day-one releases. The highlight is April 21’s Vampire Crawlers, which is going to blow your socks off, but it’s just one among many new and classic titles, including the latest from Lost Records‘ creator Don’t Nod, and a multiplayer pottery-based brawler by Double Fine.

Joining poncle’s Vampire Survivors follow-up on the 21st are the machine-building RPG Little Rocket Lab and the exquisitely named Sopa: Tale of the Stolen Potato, a narrative adventure set in a South American pantry, both originally released on PC on Oct 7, 2025.

Come the 23rd we get another day-one release with Kiln, the latest game from Pyschonauts team Double Fine. This one’s an online multiplayer brawler about sentient ceramics, where your skills in combat are based on the kind of pot you craft on a pottery wheel. Which sounds appropriately bonkers.

There’s then a five-day hiatus until another brand new game, Aphelion. This is the newest game from Don’t Nod, the folks originally behind Life Is Strange and more recently responsible for hits Jusant and Lost Records, this time offering us a hardcore science-fiction adventure which it says fuses exploration and stealth to the studio’s usual storytelling.

The next day, on April 29, we also get 2023’s extremely popular F.E.A.R.-style shooter Trepang2, and then the 30th sees the arrival on PC of a preview version of Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era, the hopefully triumphant return of the long-abandoned turn-based strategy series that began in 1995. We last saw a HOMM in 2015 with the lackluster seventh entry, so here’s hoping new developers Unfrozen can restore the old glory as it returns to the stories from the first three games.

There’s yet another day-one addition on April 30 called Sledding Game, which is an adorable-looking multiplayer game about sledding frogs with wild physics. The same day also brings us TerraTech Legion, the latest entry in Payload Studio’s TerraTech universe, this time a bullet-hell vehicle-building game—quite the departure from the franchise’s usual open-world sandbox adventures.

Last of all, yet another Final Fantasy joins Game Pass on May 5, this time Final Fantasy V.

Here are all the games in a tidy list, along with the nine games vanishing on the last day of the month as well. (Play Revenge of the Savage Planet before then!)

New Additions

  • Little Rocket Lab (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 21
  • Sopa: Tale of the Stolen Potato (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – April 21
  • Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 21
  • Kiln (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 23
  • Aphelion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 28
  • Trepang2 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 29
  • Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era (Game Preview) (PC) – April 30
  • Sledding Game (Game Preview) (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30
  • TerraTech Legion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30
  • Final Fantasy V (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – May 5

Leaving April 30

  • Citizen Sleeper (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Creatures of Ava (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Endless Legend 2 (PC)
  • Goat Simulator (Cloud and Console)
  • Goat Simulator Remastered (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Hunt Showdown 1896 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • NHL 24 (EA Play) (Cloud and Console)
  • Revenge of the Savage Planet (Cloud, Console, and PC)


https://kotaku.com/xbox-game-pass-vampire-crawlers-final-fantasy-aphelion-2000689032

item.title: Ubisoft Wants New Hires To Be Familiar With Generative AI Tools
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft seems to be going all-in on generative AI (GenAI), with new job listings asking for experience in this field.

The gaming industry seems to be split on the use of AI in game development, with Take-Two CEO stating that AI tools can help create assets but not generate hits. At the same time, NetEase recently praised AI integration across the full development and gameplay cycle. Now, Ubisoft seems to be hiring people with expertise in these tools.

Spotted by Tech4Gamers, new job listings from Ubisoft specifically seek candidates with experience in generative AI tools and models. One such listing is for a Technical Art Director position at Ubisoft Annecy, aimed at candidates who will contribute to an unannounced AAA project.

The qualifications section of this listing clearly states that the Technical Art Director must be “proficient” in generative AI tools, including ComfyUI, MidJourney, NanoBanana, and Hunyuan. Additionally, the candidate should be “comfortable working with” generative AI models like Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT, and others.

There is also a similar job opening for a Prompt Specialist at Ubisoft Paris. The job description states, “By joining us in this role, you will become part of a multidisciplinary team (comprising data, programming, game and narrative design, animation, etc.), guided by a simple yet ambitious question: among all the possibilities offered by generative AI, which ones are truly interesting and fun for gameplay?”

Additionally, the qualifications require experience with models such as GPT-4, Gemini, Claude, Qwen, SentenceBERT, Llama, Mistral, and others.

In other news, the laid-off workers from Ubisoft’s Halifax branch have voted in favor of a settlement with the company. Additionally, Insider Gaming has exclusively revealed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will release this July. What are your thoughts on Ubisoft seeking generative AI experience in new hires? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Forza Horizon 6 reportedly already selling 500,000 copies on Steam. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: The Elden Ring Movie Has A Release Date And Cast, Which Includes Dark Souls II's Peter Serafinowicz
item.content:encoded:
Back in May of last year, From Software, A24, and Bandai Namco announced its intentions for an Elden Ring movie which would be written and directed by Alex Garland. Garland is known for films like Ex Machina and most recently Civil War, but he is no stranger to the video game world having written Enslaved: Odyssey to the West and DmC: Devil May Cry for Ninja Theory.

Today we learned some new details about the movie with the reveal of its cast and release date, which is March 3, 2028. Production officially begins on the film in the coming months. The press release also confirms the film is being "Created under the guidance of FromSoftware’s Hidetaka Miyazaki."

You can check out the full cast list below, which leads with Kit Connor, who has been rumored to star in the movie for some time.

  • Kit Connor (Warfare, Heartstopper)
  • Ben Whishaw (Skyfall, Paddington)
  • Cailee Spaeny (Alien: Romulus, Civil War)
  • Tom Burke (Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga, Black Bag)
  • Havana Rose Liu (Bottoms)
  • Sonoya Mizuno (Ex Machina)
  • Jonathan Pryce (The Two Popes)
  • Ruby Cruz (Willow, Bottoms)
  • Nick Offerman (The Last of Us)
  • John Hodgkinson
  • Jefferson Hall
  • Emma Laird
  • Peter Serafinowicz

Arguably the most interesting casting is the inclusion of Peter Serafinowicz. Serafinowicz is best known as the voice of Darth Maul in Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace and playing The Tick in Amazon's live-action adaptation of the comic, but he is also a noted Dark Souls fan. Along with posting about the original Dark Souls so much that his followers accused him of being paid by Bandai Namco, he also used his clout to elbow his way into Dark Souls II where he played Pate. Of the announced cast so far, he is the only actor who has appeared in a From Software game.

Follow the link for Game Informer's review of Elden Ring, which received the rare 10 out of 10.



https://gameinformer.com/2026/04/20/the-elden-ring-movie-has-a-release-date-and-cast-which-includes-dark-souls-iis-peter
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball: Sparking! Zero evolves this summer with Super Limit Breaking NEO expansion DLC
item.content:encoded:
A new battle system update is coming too.

Dragon Ball fans are eating well today, especially after the announcement of a third entry in the Xenoverse series that's set to arrive next year. But what about Sparking! Zero, the impressive 2024 arena fighter that acted as a spiritual successor of sorts to the Tenkaichi series?

Well, it hasn't stopped receiving small content drops, but publisher Bandai Namco and developer Spike Chunsoft have just announced a far more substantial content drop is coming this summer in the shape of Super Limit Breaking NEO, which feels like a mouthful when added to the base game's title. Watch the first trailer for the big DLC here:

Over 30 additional fighters are joining the roster after fans demanded more variety instead of a deluge of Vegeta versions. The newcomers include Super 17, Bardock (Super Saiyan), Champa, and Vegeta (GT), among others, "with several characters making their Budokai Tenkaichi series debut for the first time." Even General Blue and Mighty Mask are showing up!

Extra customisation is at the centre of the refreshed experience too, with "more than 20 new options" letting players tinker with costumes and skills to create unique battle styles or "recreate iconic Dragon Ball scenes" straight from the shows and movies. It all should play well with four new stages, including Kami's Palace and the pink Kame House.

Super Limit Breaking NEO also appears to be directly addressing the complaints about a lack of more PvE modes by adding the new Limit Breaker Journey mode, where all players can "progress through branching scenarios, fights, and events, train characters through battle, earn unique rewards, and carry growth into online play." As many fighter series have slowly stepped away from meaty solo offerings, it's good to see a developer (other than NetherRealm) pay attention to single-player grind enjoyers.

Last but not least, the free update set to arrive alongside this paid DLC will rework the battle system to make it faster and more brutal, with allies coming in with assists through "Chain Blasts" and the "Sparking! Boost" bar adding a new layer of buffs to your attacks. More details should be coming soon, but it's safe to say most players will appreciate having more combat tools to play with.

If Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 and Sparking! Zero Super Limit Breaking NEO weren't enough excitement for one day, Bandai Namco also dropped trailers for Xenoverse 2's final DLC as well as the new Goku SS4 version for FighterZ.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/dragon-ball-sparking-zero-super-limit-breaking-neo-dlc-announced

item.title: Cast and Release Date Announced for the Elden Ring Film Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Nearly a year ago, Bandai Namco announced a film adaptation of Elden Ring, with Alex Garland, the writer-director behind Ex Machina and Annihilation taking those roles here. They’ve just announced that production will begin this spring, and confirmed the cast and release date.

The Elden Ring movie adaptation is set for release on March 3, 2028. It will be filmed for IMAX, which seems fitting for all of those larger than life bosses. 

Set to star are Kit Connor, Ben Whishaw, Cailee Spaeny, Tom Burke, Havana Rose Liu, Sonoya Mizuno, Jonathan Pryce, Ruby Cruz, Nick Offerman, John Hodgkinson, Jefferson Hall, Emma Laird, and Peter Serafinowicz. While none of the roles these actors are playing were announced with the list, Kit Connor is playing our Tarnished protagonist in the dark fantasy adaptation. No word on who the others are playing yet, but with production starting, we'll learn more later.

Director Alex Garland has worked with several in the cast before, like Cailee Spaeny, who he directed in the film Civil War and Sonoya Mizuno, in Ex Machina. After playing Elden Ring, Garland was instrumental in getting an adaptation to happen, first with the head of film at A24 (which is making the film), which led to a deal with FromSoftware.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/cast-and-release-date-announced-for-the-elden-ring-film-adaptation-2000137848

item.title: Dang, We Got An Elden Ring Movie Release Date Before The Switch 2 Port
item.content:encoded:
Tarnishing cinemas in 2028

Bandai Namco has announced that the A24 movie adaptation Elden Ring will release on 3rd March 2028. It will also be filmed for IMAX, so expect some sweeping landscape shots to feature throughout.

This means that, yes, we've received a firm release date for the Alex Garland-directed movie before the upcoming Switch 2 port of the original game. What the heck is going on..? Regardless, full production is set to commence in Spring 2026 (so basically any day now), and the full cast has also been confirmed:

- Kit Connor (Warfare, Heartstopper)
- Ben Whishaw (Skyfall, Paddington)
- Cailee Spaeny (Alien: Romulus, Civil War)
- Tom Burke (Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga, Black Bag)
- Havana Rose Liu (Bottoms)
- Sonoya Mizuno (Ex Machina)
- Jonathan Pryce (The Two Popes)
- Ruby Cruz (Willow, Bottoms)
- Nick Offerman (The Last of Us)
- John Hodgkinson
- Jefferson Hall
- Emma Laird
- Peter Serafinowicz

Elden Ring: Tarnished Edition is still expected to launch on the Switch 2 in 2026, though it's now been over a year since the debut trailer was showcased during the Switch 2 Direct in April 2025. It's since been playable at numerous gaming events, but Bandai Namco has refrained from providing a firm release date.

Pre-orders for the game opened up on Amazon, indicating that the Game-Key Card release will be priced at $79.99.

Will you be checking out the Elden Ring movie in 2028? Let us know your thoughts with a comment down below.

[source en.bandainamcoent.eu]

item.title: Liquid Swords’ Samson Arrives on Consoles in Fall 2026, Flurry of Updates With Bug/Performance Fixes Coming in the Meantime
item.content:encoded:
Samson: A Tyndalston Story is the debut game from developer Liquid Swords, the studio founded by Christofer Sundberg, the founder of Avalanche Studios and one of the creators of the Just Cause series. Now, after a less-than-ideal launch earlier this month, Sundberg and the team are committed to making Samson work, with a flurry of bug and technical fixes on the way, and a console release scheduled for its 2026 roadmap.

Samson was meant to be a smaller-scaled, double-A sized game, according to Liquid Swords, but even within that scope, the game still launched with a lot of technical issues. It's flurry of bugs mixed with repetitive gameplay is what brought the game down several notches in my review, despite its story and gameplay structure all adding interesting stakes and tension to the whole ordeal.

Thankfully, Liquid Swords has been quick to release a couple of updates already, with more soon on the way. It's third major update, coming on April 22, looks to be one of the most significant updates yet after its first two updates addressed instances that would cause the game to crash and some progression-blocking bugs.

This next update will add more performance fixes, improvements to combat, a refinement of the time trials, and a lot more bug fixes. The console launch, which is the last thing listed on the roadmap, is set to arrive sometime in Fall 2026, which seems more than feasible for Liquid Swords to reach, especially if it keeps up the pace it is currently working at with these updates.

The third update arriving this coming Wednesday is the lastest one in just as many weeks. A fourth update will arrive next week, and a fifth update the week after. The studio also has new content scheduled to arrive, and hopefully between the bug fixes and new content, it won't be very long before Samson is a much better game than what I got to play ahead of and at its launch.

It's the kind of redemption arc you want to see, especially when it's coming from a small independent team. Hopefully, Liquid Swords will be able to pull it off, so that it's running without issue by the time it arrives on current-gen consoles (or ideally well before that console launch arrives).

For more on Samson: A Tyndalston Story, you can check out my full review and my interview with Christofer Sundberg and Donald Young on how the game looked to achieve its "intensity over scale" design mantra.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/samson-ps5-xbox-series-release-date-liquid-swords-2026-roadmap/

item.title: Elden Ring Live Action Film Gets A Release Date And A Roundtable Hold’s-Worth Of New Cast Members
item.content:encoded:
A24’s Elden Ring film just got a release date several years down the road, as well as enough new cast members added to the roster to populate an entire Roundtable Hold and then some.

As shared by The Hollywood Reporter, we’ll see Elden Ring hit theaters on March 3, 2028. When it arrives, it will include the acting talents of Tom Burke, Havana Rose Liu, Sonoya Mizuno, Jonathan Pryce, Ruby Cruz, John Hodgkinson, Jefferson Hall, Emma Laird and Peter Serafinowicz, all new to the cast list today. They will join the handful of actors that have already been announced: Kit Connor, Ben Whishaw, Cailee Spaeny and Nick Offerman.

Unfortunately, we have no information whatsoever on what characters any of these actors will be portraying. We also do not really know how much or which bits of Elden Ring‘s winding and purposefully messy plot the film will focus on, so it’s difficult to make guesses. I feel pretty safe speculating that we’ll likely be following a Tarnished on their journey through The Lands Between and will likely see major highlights along the way, such as battles with Godrick the Grafted and Starscourge Radahn. We’ll probably also visit the Roundtable Hold and the folks hanging around there. But even that’s not for certain! The film is being directed by Ex Machina and Civil War‘s Alex Garland, and its production team includes Peter Rice, Andrew Macdonald, Allon Reich, Vince Gerardis, and George R. R. Martin. Martin being onboard is especially interesting since he worked on the worldbuilding for Elden Ring originally, which suggests to me that whatever we’re going to see necessitated an expert on the history of The Lands Between.

Despite the difficulty of guessing who any of these folks might be, I took a stab at the new cast members’ roles as follows:

Tom Burke has appeared in a number of BBC series such as The Musketeers and War & Peace, but may be best known to wider audiences as Praetorian Jack in Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga. He’s got a rugged handsome thing going on, so I could see him showing up as Blaidd the Half-Wolf or maybe even Radagon.

Havana Rose Liu is a model and actor who is best-known as Isabel in Bottoms. She’s giving off some Fia, Melina, or Malenia energy to me.

Sonoya Mizuno was Araminta in Crazy Rich Asians and has also been in La La Land, the live-action Beauty and the Beast, and is currently portraying Mysaria in HBO’s House of the Dragon. She also has some Melina energy going on, but would also feel right as Queen Marika or the witch Ranni.

Sir Jonathan Pryce has a long, award-laden career on stage as well as in film and television, with roles in films like Pirates of the Caribbean and in TV series like Game of Thrones, where he played the High Sparrow. He’s also appeared in one video game: he was Field Marshall Robert Bingham in Command & Conquer: Red Alert 3. Pryce has the kind of long theatrical resume with loads of Shakespeare on it that makes one perfect for a FromSoftware game, so I’d not be surprised to see him show up in a dramatic and prominent role like Sir Gideon Ofnir.

Ruby Cruz has been in Bottoms as Hazel Callahan and also played Princess Kit in the short-lived Willow revival series. She might show up as Roderika the spirit tuner, or maybe Millicent. Or, if the film takes a darker turn, perhaps Hyetta, the blind priestess with a taste for Shabriri Grapes.

John Hodgkinson is another Shakespearean actor with a long career onstage and plenty of TV and film credits as well. Could this man scream “Curse you, Bayle!” to the heavens with the energy necessary to shake a movie theater? Maybe!

Jefferson Hall was Hugh in Game of Thrones and plays both Jason and Tyland Lannister in House of the Dragon. I could see him fitting a lot of different parts in Elden Ring, including Rogier, Kenneth Haight, or Varre. Going through all these roles is really reminding me of the sheer scope of Elden Ring and the wide cast of weird characters available both for the spotlight or for brief cameo appearances.

Okay, Google Emma Laird with me real quick if you’re not already familiar with her work in Mayor of Kingstown on Paramount or anything else, and then tell me that she’s not Malenia or Queen Marika.

Finally, Peter Serafinowicz has been in a ton of things you’ve probably seen, ranging from Shaun of the Dead to How to Train Your Dragon to Guardians of the Galaxy, and even video games like Deus Ex: Mankind Divided and LittleBigPlanet 3. He’s also got a credit for a FromSoftware game already, having played Pate in Dark Souls 2. Serafinowicz also looks and sounds like he could give a hearty “Curse you, Bayle!” with the best of them, but would make sense in any number of roles for adult men with a very wide range. He’s got a solid array of voice acting credits specifically, which leads me to think he might be voicing a character that won’t be portrayed physically by a human necessarily, such as Alexander the Warrior Jar or Boc the Seamster. Or maybe he’s Patches?

Also, literally any of these people could be the Tarnished! Or, like, the face/voice of some CG or practical monstrosity, like whatever A24 plans to do with Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy (big snake freak), Godrick the Grafted (messed-up guy who killed a bunch of people and glued their limbs to himself), Starscourge Radahn (big man, little horse), or Margit/Margott (mean noisy fellow with too many horns). Elden Ring is rife with Weird Guys! We have so many options!

The one other clue we have as to what might be going on in the Elden Ring film is a clip of the set that a fan managed to sneak earlier this month while wandering through some random field in England. The clip shows a church of Marika, which is common enough throughout Elden Ring, so it’s difficult to say which one it might be. We can hazard a guess at probably Limgrave given the landscape around is…green, and not populated by toxic red goop or irritating swamps or snow, but “Limgrave will be in the Elden Ring movie” is hardly a dramatic revelation.

Frankly, the level of mystery around this adaptation is both exciting and apropos to the already mysterious universe. I almost hope they don’t tell us much more about it ahead of that 2028 release date. I would, however, like to see the horse that will be playing Torrent. A24, please show me the horse, thank you.



https://kotaku.com/elden-ring-live-action-film-release-date-new-cast-2000689018
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Has Reportedly Already Sold 500,000 Copies on Steam
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6, the next major title for Xbox, is seemingly already performing well on Steam, reportedly selling over 500,000 copies prior to its launch.

Playground Games’ next installment to the highly revered racing series arrives next month on May 19, 2026. To celebrate it’s launch, Xbox has already unvieled limited edition accessories, which includes a controller and headset. Now, we have been informed about it’s sales numbers on one of the platforms.

Forza Horizon 6 Pre-Launch Sales Reportedly Surpass 500,000 on Steam

According to a report by Alinea Analytics, Forza Horizon 6 has already crossed 500,000 copies sold on Steam, around a month ahead of its launch. The report adds that Forza Horizon 6 is close to reaching $30 million on Steam, with regional pricing and different editions of the game taken into account. The game has also seemingly become the “best-selling Forza title on Steam pre-launch,” with more than a month left for devs to drive further sales.

The report also points out all the factors that have helped Forza Horizon 6 sell so many copies on Steam. There were three factors that have appeared to have kicked off the sales, including the map reveal, driving gameplay and game previews appearing. The surge was further boosted by the developers’ reveal of the title’s achievement list. Playground Games and Xbox have yet to officially reveal the sales figures for Forza Horizon 6.

In other news, interested readers can check out Forza Horizon 6’s complete Summer Season Japan map here. Additionally, here are the official PC system requirements for the upcoming title. What are your thoughts on Forza Horizon 6 selling over 500,000 on Steam pre-launch? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Last of Us creator stating Ellie isn’t the only immune person. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Last of Us Creator Reportedly Said Ellie Isn’t The Only Immune Person
item.content:encoded:
The creator of The Last of Us has reportedly said there is a colony of immune people he would like to explore.

Naughty Dog has been in the news recently, thanks to a claim stating that a new Uncharted game is in the works, alongside the upcoming title, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet. Now, some intriguing information has been revealed about their other massive franchise, The Last of Us.

The Last of Us Part 3 Could Explore a Colony of Immune People

In a recent interview with KIWI TALKZ, Gabriel Betancourt, a former lighting artist at Naughty Dog, discussed the concept of additional immune individuals in The Last of Us universe. Betancourt shared insights from his conversation with Neil Druckmann, head of Naughty Dog, about the decision not to make Ellie the sole immune person at the center of the story. Druckmann replied that Ellie isn’t the only one and that “several people are immune.”

Betancourt elaborated on his conversation, revealing that Druckmann mentioned, “there’s a place, there’s a whole congregation of that kind of person.” Druckmann aims to create a “more sophisticated story” featuring “multiple characters” and expanding upon this narrative. While the Naughty Dog lead has not officially commented on this topic yet, if this information is accurate, we may see this as a plot point in the upcoming game of the series, The Last of Us Part 3.

In other news, a former Naughty Dog dev who worked on Uncharted 4 says the first version of the game didn’t go down well. Additionally, The Last of Us Online was canceled a day before the public announcement, after the director spent 7 years on it. What are your thoughts on there being a colony of immune people in The Last of Us universe? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the official reveal of the limited edition Forza Horizon 6-themed Xbox accessories. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2 Lineup Announced
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft has announced the games coming to Xbox Game Pass during the rest of April 2026, and the early part of May.

As confirmed in a post on Xbox Wire, there are a number of day-one releases coming, including Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard, Kiln, Aphelion, Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era, and Sledding Game. Final Fantasy V rounds out the lineup, arriving on May 5.

Cozy, machine building RPG Little Rocket Lab (Cloud, Console, and PC) and emotional narrative adventure game Stolen Potato (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) both join Game Pass Premium on April 21, having already been on Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass. Also on April 21, Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) hits Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass as a day one launch.

On April 23. Double Fine's Kiln (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) hits Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass as a day one release, which is expected for a Microsoft game. This latest game from the team behind Psychonauts is described as a "pottery power‑fantasy celebrating both creativity and destruction."

Moving on to April 28 and we've got Aphelion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) as another day one launch across Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass. This is a third-person sci-fi adventure developed by Don’t Nod, the studio behind Jusant and Lost Records: Bloom & Rage). The story is rooted in real-world science, created in partnership with the European Space Agency.

A day later, on April 29, gory FPS Trepang2 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) hits Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, and PC Game Pass. Then, on April 30, three games hit Game Pass: Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era (Game Preview) (PC) as a day one launch on Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass, Sledding Game (Game Preview) (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC), also a day one launch on Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass, and TerraTech Legion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) on Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass.

And finally for April 2026 Wave 2, Final Fantasy V (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) joins Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, and PC Game Pass on May 5.

Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2 lineup:

  • Little Rocket Lab (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 21, Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass
  • Sopa: Tale of the Stolen Potato (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – April 21, Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass
  • Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 21, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Kiln (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 23, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Aphelion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – April 28, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Trepang2 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 29, Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, and PC Game Pass
  • Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era (Game Preview) (PC) – April 30, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Sledding Game (Game Preview) (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • TerraTech Legion (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – April 30, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Final Fantasy V (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) – May 5, Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass

As always, a number of games are set to leave Game Pass this month. You can save up to 20% off your purchase if you buy them.

Leaving Xbox Game Pass on April 30:

  • Citizen Sleeper (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Creatures of Ava (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Endless Legend 2 (PC)
  • Goat Simulator (Cloud and Console)
  • Goat Simulator Remastered (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Hunt Showdown 1896 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • NHL 24 (EA Play) (Cloud and Console)
  • Revenge of the Savage Planet (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

item.title: Beast of Reincarnation pre-orders now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Available in standard and Deluxe editions.

Physical and digital pre-orders for “one-person, one-dog action RPG” Beast of Reincarnation are now available, publisher Fictions and developer Game Freak announced.

The game will be available in the following editions:

  • Physical Editions
    • Standard Edition (PS5, Xbox Series) – $59.99
      • A copy of the game
    • Deluxe Edition (PS5) – $69.99
      • A copy of the game
      • Black and Brown Shiba Skins for Koo
      • “Oni’s Hat”  for Emma
      • Emma’s sword “Big Dipper”
      • 100,000 Amber (in-game currency)
      • A number of vegetable seedlings!
  • Digital Editions
    • Standard Edition (PS5, Xbox Series, PC) – $59.99 / £49.99 / €54.99 / ¥7,979
      • A copy of the game
    • Deluxe Edition (PS5, Xbox Series, PC) – $69.99 / £57.99 / €64.99 / ¥8,989
      • A copy of the game
      • Black and Brown Shiba Skins for Koo
      • “Oni’s Hat”  for Emma
      • Emma’s sword “Big Dipper”
      • 100,000 Amber (in-game currency)
      • A number of vegetable seedlings!

Pre-orders for any edition include the “Brown Siba Skin” for Koo and 30,000 Amber (in-game currency).

Here is an overview of the game, via Fictions:

About

Set in a post-apocalyptic Japan, humanity’s only hope lies with Emma, an outcast shunned from society for her affliction, and Koo, the blighted dog. Together, they embark on this expansive adventure in this one-person, one-dog action RPG.

Experience a journey where you feel moments of loneliness, and also the reliability and comfort of your companion. Savor a world of impermanence, where forests suddenly burst forth amidst the wasteland. As Emma and Koo travel through this beautiful yet harsh world, their bond and abilities blossom.

Customize your playstyle with unique skill trees, gear, and spirit stones to survive this beautiful yet brutal world. Define your own playstyle through your preferred loadout, including ranged, stealthy, and aggressive combat.

In this world ravaged by blight… What will you find at the end of your journey?

Key Features

  • Emma and Koo’s Synergy – Emma and Koo create a revolutionary action RPG experience. Fight as a unit with the protagonist, Emma’s sword abilities while giving Koo commands to unleash various techniques, just like in a turn-based RPG.
  • Japan: Year 4026 – Travel through a beautiful yet harsh post-apocalyptic world. Experience the ever-changing scenery, in the blighted forest, and the Bosses that rule this world. Emma and Koo, who met in the far east, are tasked with battling Bosses from all over the world and capturing their powers in order to defeat the Beast of Reincarnation.
  • A Story Filled With Secrets – Dive into a brutal narrative where every character hides a dangerous truth. Uncover the intertwined fates of Emma and Koo in this profound story.

Beast of Reincarnation is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on August 4.

Watch a new trailer below.

Physical Edition Trailer

Japanese

item.title: Final Fantasy 14 gets a major update announced in Trail to the Heavens, bringing new story quests, raids, and job
item.content:encoded:
Final Fantasy 14 is getting a new major update in Trail to the Heavens, it's 7.5 patch which will bring new min story quests, alliance raid, limited job, and more.

Announced over the weekend, a new trailer highlighting the biggest additions in this update was released. You can view it below, maybe it'll inspire another free-time devouring MMO grind.

In terms of a list of all the new additions, you can find them below:

  • New Main Scenario Quests
  • Allied Society Capstone Quests
  • Inconceivably Further Hildibrand Adventures
  • New Custom Deliveries - Tiisol Ja
  • Duty Support Additions - Support for The Dusk Vigil & Shisui of the Violet Tides will be added
  • PvP Updates - PvP Series 11 begins, with adjustments to existing PvP actions and the addition of a new Crystalline Conflict arena: the Archeia Harmonia
  • New Dungeon - The Clyteum
  • New Alliance Raid - Echoes of Vana'diel, Part 3 - Windurst: The Third Walk
  • New Trial - Enuo - Includes both Normal and Extreme difficulties
  • New Unreal Trial - Shinryu's Domain (Unreal): Take on the mighty Shinryu at level 100
  • New Ultimate Raid
  • New Occult Crescent Update - New zone with new monsters, Critical Encounters, increased knowledge level, and multiple new Phantom Jobs
  • New Limited Job - Beastmaster - Capture beasts and call upon them to fight at your side!
  • Phantom Weapon updates
  • Ocean Fishing Update - New route (towards Thavnair)
  • Cosmic Exploration: New Planet - Auxsia - A new planet for intrepid crafters and gatherers to explore, as well as additional updates and changes
  • Housing Update - Increased limits for interior and exterior furnishings, new interior designs, and more
  • Armoire Update - A dramatic increase to the number of items that can be stored
  • Dye System Update
  • Miscellaneous Updates - Companion Chocobo system update, Crafting updates, Gold Saucer updates and more.

This update comes in the midst of Final Fantasy 14's Dawntrail expansion, a new narrative arc that initially had players torn having come from the many highs of Endwalker. Still, in time, it has managed to pick up Steam, listening to community feedback as the MMO continues its life over a decade since its initial release.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/final-fantasy-14-trail-to-the-heavens-new-story-quests-raids-job

IGN is partnering with ID@Xbox again to deliver a new showcase on 23rd April, and we've received the first details about the games coming to Xbox and PC that will be present.
